Title: Chemical genetic screening identifies nalacin as an inhibitor of GH3 amido synthetase for auxin conjugation
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates plant growth and development, especially root architecture.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of all eight group II GH3 genes confers resistance to nalacin and abolishes nalacin-induced changes in IAA and IAA conjugates, demonstrating that group II GH3s are the major in vivo targets underlying auxin-like phenotypes.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The △8gh3 mutant was resistant to nalacin-induced root and hypocotyl phenotypes. 2. Nalacin no longer altered endogenous IAA, IAA-Asp, or IAA-Glu levels in △8gh3. 3. Transcriptomic responses to nalacin were largely lost in △8gh3, confirming group II GH3s as major in vivo targets.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH3 family acyl acid amido synthetases
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Higher-order mutant analysis; metabolite profiling; RNA-seq
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad suppression of group II GH3 activity causes strong developmental alterations associated with elevated auxin.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents combined genetic evidence for redundant group II GH3 enzymes rather than a single gene.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highlights group II GH3 family as a redundant metabolic module suitable for chemical manipulation of auxin homeostasis.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Group II GH3-mediated IAA-amino acid conjugation
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Gretchen Hagen 3.3 acyl acid amido synthetase
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH3 family acyl acid amido synthetase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Conjugates indole-3-acetic acid to aspartate in an ATP-dependent amide formation reaction.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly inactivates/diverts active IAA into amino acid-conjugated forms, thereby reducing free IAA available for auxin responses.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates plant growth and development, including root architecture and hypocotyl growth.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Inhibition of GH3.3 by nalacin suppresses IAA-Asp formation, elevates endogenous free IAA, and promotes auxin-like developmental responses.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH3.3 was selected as a representative group II GH3 enzyme directly targeted by nalacin. 2. Nalacin directly bound GH3.3 in BLI assays. 3. Nalacin inhibited GH3.3-catalyzed IAA-Asp production with mixed-type inhibition, supporting reduced IAA conjugation as a cause of increased endogenous IAA.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rapidly induced by auxin treatment, then decreases due to negative feedback regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rapidly induced by auxin treatment, then decreases due to negative feedback regulation; nalacin causes slower, longer-lasting induction.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BLI binding assay; recombinant protein in vitro enzyme kinetics; metabolite profiling; gene expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Elevated auxin signaling is associated with inhibited primary root growth and altered root/hypocotyl architecture.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a representative enzyme for one subgroup of Arabidopsis group II GH3s.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Group II GH3 inhibition offers a chemical strategy to transiently raise auxin levels and modify root traits without directly applying auxin.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Group II GH3-mediated IAA-amino acid conjugation
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Gretchen Hagen 3.6 acyl acid amido synthetase
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH3 family acyl acid amido synthetase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes ATP-dependent conjugation of IAA to aspartate.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly inactivates active IAA by converting it to amino acid conjugates.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates plant architecture, root development, and hypocotyl elongation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Nalacin/D4 inhibition of GH3.6 reduces IAA conjugation, increases free IAA, and contributes to auxin-like effects on root and hypocotyl development.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH3.6 was a central enzymatic target used for biochemical and structural dissection of nalacin action. 2. Nalacin directly bound GH3.6 and inhibited IAA-Asp formation with strong mixed-type inhibition. 3. The crystal structure of GH3.6-AMP-D4 and docking of nalacin revealed the molecular basis of inhibition. 4. GH3.6 was described as a key player in primary root growth, correlating with different effects of nalacin and D4.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Nalacin induced GH3.6 among auxin-responsive genes in transcriptome profiling.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for local auxin conjugation; described as a key player in primary root growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BLI binding assay; recombinant enzyme kinetics; crystal structure (PDB 7VKA); molecular docking; metabolite profiling; transcriptomics
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nalacin treatment; D4 treatment; IAA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced GH3.6-mediated auxin inactivation can inhibit primary root growth while promoting adventitious rooting and hypocotyl elongation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Crystal structure deposited as AtGH3.6-AMP-D4 complex (PDB: 7VKA).
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A prime target for chemical or genetic modulation of auxin homeostasis; may be engineered into nalacin-tolerant GH3 variants for herbicide tolerance or precise auxin control.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Group II GH3-mediated IAA-amino acid conjugation
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Gretchen Hagen 3.17 acyl acid amido synthetase
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH3 family acyl acid amido synthetase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes ATP-dependent conjugation of IAA to glutamate.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly channels IAA into an inactive amino acid-conjugated form, lowering free IAA.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Nalacin inhibition of GH3.17 suppresses IAA-Glu formation and contributes to elevated endogenous IAA and auxin-like phenotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH3.17 was chosen as a representative group II GH3 and directly bound by nalacin. 2. Nalacin inhibited GH3.17-catalyzed IAA-Glu production with mixed-type inhibition. 3. These data support group II GH3 inhibition as the biochemical basis of nalacin-induced auxin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BLI binding assay; recombinant protein enzyme kinetics; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Perturbation of GH3.17 activity contributes to altered auxin balance and developmental remodeling.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as representative of a third subgroup within Arabidopsis group II GH3s.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports group II GH3s as targets for chemical modulation of auxin homeostasis.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: JASMONATE RESISTANT 1 (JAR1) / GH3.11 acyl acid amido synthetase
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH3 family acyl acid amido synthetase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes ATP-dependent conjugation of jasmonic acid to isoleucine.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Indirect to paper’s main target; off-target GH3 substrate branch affected by nalacin.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive jasmonate involved in jasmonate signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Nalacin suppresses GH3.11-catalyzed JA-Ile production in vitro and decreases endogenous JA-Ile in planta, but this was not responsible for the root/hypocotyl phenotypes emphasized in the study.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Nalacin inhibited GH3.11/JAR1 in vitro with mixed-type inhibition. 2. Nalacin lowered endogenous JA-Ile in Arabidopsis. 3. jar1-1 responded similarly to nalacin as Col-0, so GH3.11 inhibition was not the basis of the focal auxin-like developmental phenotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assay; endogenous JA-Ile measurement; mutant comparison with jar1-1
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible effects on JA-related processes beyond the assays performed.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because nalacin also targets this GH3 family member, but authors explicitly focus mechanistically on IAA-conjugating group II GH3s.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Selectivity improvement will be important to minimize JA-related off-target effects.
